programmes.show_description: During the 1922 Turkish Civil War, two American adventurers and a band of mercenaries guard a covert transport through hostile territory. Tony Curtis and Charles Bronson star.
3/10. Marcus is on the hunt for the perfect chicken and gets a lesson in developing his own mushroom patch.
Archaeologists Alex and Peter and historian Ruth try Edwardian life for a year. The team get to grips with the revolutionary technologies of the age.
10/20. An antiques expert, a market trader and an upcycler compete to make the most money. Roo Irvine, Danny Sebastian and Kevin Paxton head to the Scottish Borders.

In Cyprus, Tonia Buxton sets out on a fishing trip with her father, to catch local octopus. It's not always easy to catch them, but well worth it when she cooks up octopus and red wine stew (S1/E7)
In this episode, Tonia Buxton pays a visit to Greece's capital Athens. She cooks avgolemono soup, which is a filling chicken and rice dish, and souvlaki - Greece's favourite fast food.(S1/E8)
Scottish-born actor Alan Cumming explores the picturesque Outer Hebrides of Scotland, touring the Western Isles from Barra to Stornoway on Lewis, Eriskay and the Callanish stones.

A detective film set in seedy seventies London. A serial killer is targeting women who all appeared in the magazine Playbirds . Contains adult scenes.

2/4. A 19th-century Japanese silver punchbowl, a boule table and a John Constable painting go under the hammer. And will a 1930s Philco radio make a sound profit?
DIY expert Dave Wellman visits a couple in Southampton, who have an unfinished bathroom and building site for a home. 1/20
Dave Wellman who demonstrates how to create more space in a boxroom and make it into a into a proper bedroom. 2/10
Richard returns to Scotland to embark on tour of the Highlands and Islands. The journey offers him the opportunity to discover more about his homeland and reflect on his life.
Archaeologists Alex and Peter and historian Ruth try Edwardian life for a year. Alex and Peter go down a copper mine, just a few hundred yards from their cottage.
5/10. Marcus finds that getting his own ducks proves more of an adventure than he anticipated. He also gets a lesson on what is edible and growing wild in the hedgerows and bushes.

Stories of people who have experienced infidelity, the reasons why people stray outside their relationship and if a relationship can ever survive.
Solomon Watkins' quirky take as he interviews Michael, owner of the world's most unique collection of Really Thick Glasses. Funded and supported by Together TV and Cat Flap Media.
Carry On-style comedy curio. A divorced man teaches girls how to swindle rich men - and in return he gets a cut of their takings. Contains nudity and 60s social attitudes.
